manual_dives.title: Natural Language → 3D Scenes: The Pipeline That Didn't Exist 6 Months Ago
manual_dives.thesis: MCP + Blender + Tripo + Claude Code together create a natural language 3D scene builder — describe what you want, get a full 3D scene. This was impossible before MCP existed.

connection_map.DHoPsMOxIRl: Blender MCP + Tripo: the 2D→3D bridge via natural language
connection_map.DIcw6N6snq1: Hunyuan3D-2: free image-to-3D mesh generation (the geometry engine)
connection_map.DXKFrnQEfEQ: Claude Code + Higgsfield: automated motion design + 3D content
connection_map.DFaChZeMdn1: TouchDesigner + MediaPipe: real-time body→3D interaction layer

manual_dives.action_sketch: Set up Blender MCP server → connect to Claude Code → use Tripo/Hunyuan3D for mesh gen → describe scenes in plain English → get rigged, lit 3D environments. Add TouchDesigner for live interaction.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Six months ago, creating a 3D scene meant hours in Blender learning hotkeys, topology, and lighting. Now you type 'a cozy cabin in snow with warm interior lighting' and MCP bridges Claude Code to Blender, Tripo generates the meshes, and Hunyuan3D handles the geometry. The Model Context Protocol is the glue — it lets Claude manipulate Blender like a human operator, but faster. This isn't just automation; it's a new creative medium where language IS the modeling tool. The barrier to 3D went from years of training to a well-written sentence.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Blender (free), Claude Code subscription, Tripo API key (free tier available), MCP server setup (~15 min)

manual_dives.title: The Autonomous Brand Machine: Claude Code as Your Entire Marketing Team
manual_dives.thesis: Claude Code + Playwright MCP + Higgsfield + knowledge graphs = a system that generates ads, UGC, 3D content, and posts it — autonomously. One person runs what used to need a 5-person team.

connection_map.DXKFrnQEfEQ: Claude Code + Higgsfield = automated motion design + ad creation
connection_map.DW4OC7qDj_w: Graphify knowledge graph: Claude maps your entire brand's assets
connection_map.DW2J0eUDKHM: Auto-approve mode: Claude runs the full pipeline unattended
connection_map.DVfZdeqDvyl: iOS Shortcuts integration: trigger campaigns from your phone
connection_map.DWo1P-KDpMf: Obsidian knowledge system: the business brain that feeds the machine

manual_dives.action_sketch: Build Obsidian knowledge base of brand assets → connect via Graphify → Claude Code generates ads/UGC via Higgsfield → Playwright MCP posts to social → iOS Shortcuts for mobile triggers. Total cost: Claude subscription.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: The traditional marketing team — copywriter, designer, videographer, social media manager, strategist — costs $30K+/month. This pipeline replaces all five roles with one Claude Code session that runs autonomously. Playwright MCP handles posting, Higgsfield generates motion content, the Obsidian knowledge graph ensures brand consistency across everything generated. The key insight: auto-approve mode means Claude doesn't stop to ask permission. You set the strategy, the machine executes 24/7. One person with this stack produces more content than a 5-person team, and it gets smarter each cycle because CLAUDE.md captures what works.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Claude Code, Playwright MCP, Higgsfield account, Obsidian vault, social media accounts with API access

manual_dives.title: Physics Models → Market Alpha: When Scientists Become Traders
manual_dives.thesis: 26 posts map physics/math models to trading — avalanche theory, Ising models, phase space reconstruction. The meta-insight: the quant edge isn't in finance, it's in importing models from other fields.

connection_map.DTLkCVXE7O6: Phase Space Reconstruction: topology from physics → BTC volatility
connection_map.DTx3Bi3E-hp: Avalanche Model: earthquake science → market crash prediction
connection_map.DTsn_IKE06E: Ising Model: magnetism → herd behavior modeling
connection_map.DW6uAQdk1r0: Heston Model: stochastic vol → realistic options pricing
connection_map.DU8m2RCE1Ea: Omori's Law: seismology aftershock math → post-crash dynamics
connection_map.DUx9t_FE0CY: Wasserstein Distance: optimal transport → backtest-live gap
connection_map.DT-rdimDamA: Gold Quant Agent: 6M data points + XGBoost = AI trading system
connection_map.DVi7KgKjZbM: Monte Carlo: simulation proves the math works

manual_dives.action_sketch: Study the cross-disciplinary models. Build a signal ensemble that combines 3+ physics-derived indicators. Backtest with Monte Carlo validation. Deploy as an AI agent that runs daily.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Wall Street's edge has always come from importing ideas other traders haven't seen. Renaissance Technologies hired physicists, not MBAs. This catalog maps the same playbook: Ising models from magnetism predict herd behavior, Omori's earthquake law models post-crash dynamics, phase space reconstruction from topology reveals hidden BTC patterns. The meta-insight isn't any single model — it's that the best alpha comes from fields nobody on Wall Street is reading. Each physics paper is a potential trading signal that quant Twitter hasn't priced in yet. The 26 posts here form a complete curriculum for building a cross-disciplinary signal ensemble.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Python + NumPy/SciPy, basic statistics, willingness to read physics papers, backtesting framework (backtrader or custom)

manual_dives.title: The Self-Improving AI Workspace: Claude Code That Gets Smarter Every Session
manual_dives.thesis: CLAUDE.md + primer.md + memory hooks + Obsidian + parallel worktrees = an AI dev environment that learns your preferences, remembers context, and runs multiple streams simultaneously.

connection_map.DV6QY8QjoRW: CLAUDE.md as living rulebook: self-improving instructions
connection_map.DVQ5o6YDfsp: Parallel worktrees: 10-15 Claude sessions running simultaneously
connection_map.DVzvsgijOLT: primer.md: live session state that persists across context resets
connection_map.DXCu2p4EQe3: Obsidian second brain: 3 systems you need for persistent AI memory
connection_map.DWo1P-KDpMf: 3D knowledge graph: your entire business spinning in real time
connection_map.DWoxW7pjjkH: Claude as tutor: PDF guides generated from your knowledge base

manual_dives.action_sketch: Set up CLAUDE.md with self-improvement rules → add primer.md for session continuity → connect Obsidian vault → enable parallel worktrees for concurrent work → watch the system compound over weeks.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Most people use Claude as a chatbot — ask a question, get an answer, context dies. This stack makes Claude a persistent collaborator that remembers your preferences, coding style, and project history across sessions. CLAUDE.md is the living rulebook (it self-improves when you correct mistakes), primer.md carries session state across context resets, parallel worktrees let you run 10-15 Claude sessions simultaneously on different parts of the same codebase. The compounding effect is real: after a week, Claude knows your patterns better than a new hire would after a month. After a month, it's like having a senior engineer who's read every line of your code.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Claude Code subscription, git (for worktrees), Obsidian (optional but recommended), ~30 min initial setup

manual_dives.title: Napkin Sketch → Production Asset: The 5-Tool Creative Pipeline
manual_dives.thesis: A hand-drawn sketch can become a production-ready 3D animation in under an hour by chaining Procreate → Magnific AI → 3DAI Studio → Move AI → final render. Each tool handles one transformation step.

connection_map.C5010-Kh1Mv: The full pipeline: sketch → upscale → 3D → motion capture → animate
connection_map.DDFdSEDtKZV: Kling Motion Brush: direct up to 5 characters in one AI video
connection_map.DIcw6N6snq1: Hunyuan3D-2: skip the 3D modeling step entirely with AI
connection_map.DHTvblkRgZG: AI rotoscoping: mask yourself into your own animations

manual_dives.action_sketch: Draw in Procreate → upscale with Magnific → generate 3D mesh with Hunyuan3D or 3DAI → capture motion with Move AI → composite with Kling or Runway. Total time: ~45 minutes per asset.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: The gap between 'I have an idea' and 'I have a production asset' used to be weeks of specialized work across multiple disciplines. This pipeline collapses it to under an hour by chaining five AI tools, each handling one transformation: sketch → upscale → 3D mesh → motion → final render. The key is that each tool is best-in-class at its specific step, and they hand off cleanly. A napkin doodle at lunch becomes a rigged, animated 3D character by dinner. For indie creators, this is the difference between having ideas and shipping them.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Procreate or any drawing app, Magnific AI, Hunyuan3D-2 or 3DAI Studio, Kling or Runway account

manual_dives.title: Body → Machine → Art: Real-Time Interactive Installations Anyone Can Build
manual_dives.thesis: Kinect/MediaPipe body tracking + TouchDesigner + Unreal Engine = interactive art installations that respond to human movement. Previously needed a studio budget; now runs on a gaming laptop.

connection_map.DVoReJNCGxT: DATA CONTROL: Kinect + TouchDesigner + Unreal for scientific viz
connection_map.DFaChZeMdn1: MediaPipe + TouchDesigner: body tracking without expensive hardware
connection_map.DW9OgzCFcGY: Mirror systems: engineering meets art in mechanical installations
connection_map.CvsDXJbJh6O: Cymatics: sound → physical form → visual art
connection_map.CwNnre_Ih9l: Al Bahar Towers: architecture that responds to sunlight

manual_dives.action_sketch: MediaPipe for body tracking (free, runs on webcam) → pipe data to TouchDesigner → drive visuals in Unreal or project with a $200 projector. Gallery-quality interactive art for under $500 in hardware.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Interactive art installations used to require a $50K+ budget: custom hardware, specialized software, a team of creative technologists. Now MediaPipe runs body tracking on a $30 webcam, TouchDesigner handles the visual pipeline, and a $200 projector fills a wall. The total cost dropped 99%. This democratization means independent artists can build gallery-quality interactive work in their apartment. The posts here map the complete stack from body input to visual output, including real examples of installations that respond to movement, sound, and light.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Webcam, TouchDesigner (free for non-commercial), projector ($200+), laptop with decent GPU

manual_dives.title: The One-Person Video Studio: AI Tools That Replace a Production Team
manual_dives.thesis: Runway + Kling + rotoscoping + motion brush = professional video production without a crew, studio, or actors. Direct scenes with text prompts and motion controls.

connection_map.DDIAME6MhzY: Runway node canvas: non-linear AI video editing
connection_map.DDFdSEDtKZV: Kling Motion Brush: direct character movement in AI video
connection_map.C_8ia4lsMB3: Runway Gen-3: precise video-to-video motion control
connection_map.C91jKVfxtG1: Kling free rollout: no more waitlists, everyone has access
connection_map.DHTvblkRgZG: AI rotoscoping: insert yourself into any scene

manual_dives.action_sketch: Script with Claude → generate base footage with Runway/Kling → direct characters with motion brush → rotoscope yourself in → edit on the node canvas → export. Cost: ~$30/month in AI tools.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Professional video production has three bottlenecks: talent (actors/crew), equipment (cameras/lighting/studio), and post-production (editing/VFX). AI tools collapse all three. Runway's node canvas replaces the editing suite. Kling's motion brush replaces the director ('move this character's arm here'). Rotoscoping lets you insert yourself into any scene without a green screen. OpenScreen handles screen recording for tutorials. The result: one person with a laptop produces content that previously needed a production team. The cost dropped from $10K/video to $30/month in subscriptions.
manual_dives.prerequisites: Runway account (~$12/mo), Kling (free tier), OpenScreen (free), basic video editing concept

manual_dives.title: Code as Canvas: When Algorithms Become the Artist
manual_dives.thesis: Ant colonies, boid simulations, particle systems, and generative algorithms create art that's alive — emergent beauty from simple rules. The artist writes the rules, not the pixels.

connection_map.DVCBNQ8jCly: Ant Colony Optimization: pheromone trails become visual patterns
connection_map.DVndfhOE7A9: Boid flocking: emergent crowd behavior as living art
connection_map.DWLMdCUN1eR: LLM canvas coding: plant-creature prompts as creative constraint
connection_map.DWlrC88Db2r: Mix Universe: accidental beauty from music + code

manual_dives.action_sketch: Pick a natural system (flocking, diffusion, growth). Code it in p5.js or TouchDesigner. Add interaction (sound, body tracking, data feeds). The art emerges — you don't draw it, you evolve it.
manual_dives.why_it_matters: Traditional art is about control — every brushstroke is intentional. Generative art inverts this: you write rules, and beauty emerges from complexity. Ant colony simulations create organic trail patterns. Boid flocking algorithms produce mesmerizing crowd dynamics. Particle systems generate physics-driven visual poetry. The artist's role shifts from pixel-pusher to system designer. The posts here show the full spectrum: from mathematical simulations to creative coding gardens to accidental beauty from music+code. This is art that's alive — it never produces the same output twice.
manual_dives.prerequisites: p5.js or Processing (both free), basic programming concepts, optional: TouchDesigner for real-time interaction
